<TR>
<TD WIDTH="4%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">(1)</FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Cash equivalents primarily correspond to deposits at call with banks. </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="4%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">(2)</FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The carrying amount of the senior unsecured convertible bonds as reported above corresponds to the liability component only, since, at initial
recognition, an amount of $121 million was recorded directly in shareholders&#146; equity as the value of the equity instrument embedded in the issued convertible bonds. </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">No securities were in an unrealized loss position as at December&nbsp;31, 2014 and December&nbsp;31, 2013. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The methodologies used to estimate fair value are as follows: </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Debt securities classified as available-for-sale </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The fair value of these debt securities is estimated based upon quoted market prices for identical instruments. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Foreign exchange forward contracts, currency options and collars </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The fair value of these instruments is estimated based upon quoted market prices for similar instruments. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Marketable securities classified as available-for-sale </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The fair values of these instruments are estimated based upon market prices for identical instruments. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Equity securities classified as available-for-sale </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The fair values of these instruments are estimated based upon market prices for identical instruments. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Trading equity securities </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The fair value of these instruments is estimated based upon quoted market prices for the same instruments. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Equity securities carried at cost </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The non-recurring fair value measurement
is based on the valuation of the underlying investments on a new round of third party financing or upon liquidation. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Long-term debt and current portion of long-term debt </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The fair value of
bank loans is determined by estimating future cash flows on a borrowing-by-borrowing basis and discounting these future cash flows using the Company&#146;s incremental borrowing rates for similar types of borrowing arrangements. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The senior unsecured convertible bonds have been trading on the open market segment of the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since issuance on
July&nbsp;3, 2014. The fair value of these instruments is the observable price of the bonds on that market. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Cash and cash equivalents, accounts receivable, short-term borrowings, and accounts payable </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The carrying amounts reflected in the consolidated financial statements are reasonable estimates of fair value due to the relatively short
period of time between the origination of the instruments and their expected realization. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">F-56
</FONT></P>

Globalfoundries, MicroOLED, Soitec, Oracle, Thales and Technicolor. The related party transactions presented in the table above also include transactions between the Company and its equity-method investments as listed in Note 10. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Until the sale of its JVD shares to Ericsson on August&nbsp;2, 2013, leading to the de-recognition of its equity investment in JVD, the
Company purchased R&amp;D services from JVD ($121 million in 2013). For the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012, the total R&amp;D services purchased from ST-Ericsson AT SA amounted to $224 million and outstanding trade payables amounted to $44
million. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Company made a contribution of $0.5 million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2014 to the ST Foundation, a
non-profit organization established to deliver and coordinate independent programs in line with its mission. The Company made a contribution to ST Foundation of $0.5 million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013 and no contribution in the year
ended December&nbsp;31, 2012. Certain members of the Foundation&#146;s Board are senior members of the Company&#146;s management. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:18px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

<TD WIDTH="4%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>25.</B></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>SEGMENT INFORMATION </B></FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Company operates in two business areas: Semiconductors and Subsystems. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In
the Semiconductors business area, the Company designs, develops, manufactures and markets a broad range of products, including discrete and standard commodity components, application-specific integrated circuits (&#147;ASICs&#148;), full custom
devices and semi-custom devices and application-specific standard products (&#147;ASSPs&#148;) for analog, digital, and mixed-signal applications. In addition, the Company further participates in the manufacturing value chain of Smartcard products,
which includes the production and sale of both silicon chips and Smartcards. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Company&#146;s segments in 2014 are as
follows: </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

<TD WIDTH="3%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">&#149;</FONT></TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Other EPS. </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In the second half of 2014, the Company announced that as of the first quarter of 2015 the Digital Convergence Group (DCG) and Imaging, Bi-CMOS and Silicon Photonics (IBP) Group would be combined under
one single organization, called Digital Product Group (DPG). DPG&#146;s focus is on ASSPs addressing home gateway and set-top box, as well as FD-SOI ASICs for consumer applications; FD-SOI and mixed process ASICs, including silicon photonics,
addressing communication infrastructure; and differentiated imaging products. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">F-57
</FONT></P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In 2014, the Company revised the revenues by product line from prior periods following the
reclassification of Image Signal Processor business from IBP product line to DCG product line. In addition, the Wireless former product line has been reclassified into the DCG product line. The Company believes that the revised 2013 and 2012
revenues presentation is consistent with that of 2014 and uses these comparatives when managing the company. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In the
Subsystems business area, the Company designs, develops, manufactures and markets subsystems and modules for the telecommunications, automotive and industrial markets including mobile phone accessories, battery chargers, ISDN power supplies and
in-vehicle equipment for electronic toll payment. Based on its immateriality to its business as a whole, the Subsystems business area does not meet the requirements for a reportable segment as defined in the U.S. GAAP guidance. All the financial
values related to Subsystems including net revenues and related costs, are reported in the segment &#147;Others&#148;. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The
following tables present the Company&#146;s consolidated net revenues and consolidated operating income (loss) by product segment.&nbsp;For the computation of the segments&#146; internal financial measurements, we use certain internal rules of
allocation for the costs not directly chargeable to the segments, including cost of sales, selling, general and administrative (&#147;SG&amp;A&#148;) expenses and a part of research and development (&#147;R&amp;D&#148;) expenses. In compliance with
the Company&#146;s internal policies, certain cost items are not charged to the segments, including impairment, restructuring charges and other related closure costs, unused capacity charges, phase-out and start-up costs of certain manufacturing
facilities, certain one-time corporate items, strategic and special R&amp;D programs or other corporate-sponsored initiatives, including certain corporate-level operating expenses and certain other miscellaneous charges. In addition, depreciation
and amortization expense is part of the manufacturing costs allocated to the product segments and is neither identified as part of the inventory variation nor as part of the unused capacity charges; therefore, it cannot be isolated in the costs of
goods sold. Finally, R&amp;D grants are allocated to our product lines proportionally to the incurred R&amp;D expenses on the sponsored projects. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Net revenues by product segment: </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:12px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>
